evidence: ExplorationHandlers has seven private formatXxx() methods (formatSeedCollocate, formatDiscoveredNoun, formatCoreCollocate, formatEdge, formatAdjectiveProfile, and inline handling in buildExploreResponseBody) that reproduce the field names and rounding logic already implemented in ExplorationResult.toMap() and ComparisonResult.toMap() — two complete seam implementations for the same model-to-JSON translation

handleSemanticFieldExplore() requires the parameter key 'seeds' (plural) via HttpApiUtils.requireParam(exchange, params, 'seeds') but uses the returned value as a single seed — the param key is semantically wrong at the boundary between HTTP and domain

RelationConfig.mergeLemmaConstraint() is a private pattern-manipulation method that does the same job as PatternSubstitution.substituteCollocate() in the api package — two independent seam-translation implementations for CQL lemma injection at adjacent package boundaries

ExplorationHandlers.buildBaseExploreResponse() and buildExploreResponseBody() cleanly factor out the common parts of both explore handlers — single-seed and multi-seed paths converge to the same response structure via a shared builder pair, which is good seam discipline

SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern() delegates the fallback logic (try BCQL pattern, fall back to simple pattern) through fetchSeedCollocates() — the boundary rule is in one named method, not scattered
The QueryExecutor interface cleanly hides BlackLab internals from SemanticFieldExplorer — the explorer never imports nl.inl.blacklab, and the seam is honoured throughout the exploration package
ExploreOptions record packages the 5 exploration tuning parameters, removing a wide argument list at the SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern() boundary — this is an effective seam simplification

Error propagation across the handler→explorer boundary is inconsistent: IOException is caught and re-reported as HTTP 500, but IllegalArgumentException is caught separately and reported as 400 — this is a reasonable convention but it is only applied in the exploration handlers; SketchHandlers catch generic Exception and report it as 400 for BCQL errors and as 500 for I/O errors without a typed convention

strengths: QueryExecutor interface is a clean seam: exploration and handler packages depend only on the interface, never on BlackLab types directly
ExploreOptions record removes a 5-argument method boundary and makes the tuning contract explicit
buildBaseExploreResponse() + buildExploreResponseBody() correctly factor out common response structure shared by single-seed and multi-seed paths
resolveRelationConfig() and resolveExploreParams() extract repeated handler preambles into named boundary helpers, reducing copy-paste across exploration handlers
mid_level_elegance.issue_character: The dominant issue is duplicate translation at the model-to-JSON seam: two independent serialization paths (handler formatXxx methods and model toMap() methods) exist for the same data, and a parallel duplication appears at the CQL substitution seam (RelationConfig vs PatternSubstitution); these indicate that integration seams were added without consolidating existing translations.
mid_level_elegance.score_rationale: The query-to-explorer seam is well-designed and the response-building factoring is sound. However the codebase has two specific cross-layer translation duplications that force a developer to update multiple places for one schema change: the model serialization seam and the CQL lemma-substitution seam. Additionally the 'seeds' parameter naming inconsistency at the HTTP seam is a confusing API contract. These are not cosmetic issues — they affect maintainability of the boundary contracts. Score sits at 62: good seam infrastructure let down by unresolved translation duplication.

issues.summary: Model-to-JSON translation is duplicated: handler formatXxx() methods and model toMap() methods both fully implement the same ExplorationResult/ComparisonResult → Map conversion
evidence: ExplorationHandlers.formatDiscoveredNoun() produces {word, shared_count, similarity_score, avg_logdice, shared_collocates} — ExplorationResult.toMap() produces discovered_nouns list with the same field set and rounding
ExplorationHandlers.formatEdge() matches Edge.toMap() field for field
Any schema change (e.g. renaming 'similarity_score') must be made in both ExplorationHandlers and ExplorationResult.toMap()
issues.suggestion: Pick one translation point. The model-owned toMap()/toMap() approach keeps serialization logic co-located with the data. Remove the seven formatXxx() methods from ExplorationHandlers and replace buildExploreResponseBody() with a call to result.toMap() plus targeted response.put() for the top-level keys the handler adds (status, relation_type, parameters, seed/seeds).

issues.summary: Single-seed explore endpoint reads the parameter key 'seeds' (plural) but treats the value as a single seed — confusing API contract
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore(): String seed = HttpApiUtils.requireParam(exchange, params, 'seeds'); — the key is 'seeds' but the value is passed directly as a single seed string to semanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern(seed, ...)
The multi-seed endpoint also uses 'seeds' but correctly splits on commas: parseSeedSet(seedsStr)
An API client cannot distinguish the two endpoints by parameter names — both accept 'seeds' but with different value semantics (single vs comma-separated list)
issues.suggestion: Rename the single-seed endpoint's parameter key to 'seed' (singular) in handleSemanticFieldExplore(). Update API documentation accordingly. This makes the HTTP contract honest without requiring any internal refactoring.

issues.summary: CQL lemma-into-token substitution is independently implemented in RelationConfig (config package) and PatternSubstitution (api package)
evidence: RelationConfig.mergeLemmaConstraint(): extracts xpos via CqlUtils.extractConstraintAttribute, then builds [lemma="..." & xpos="..."] — for head injection
PatternSubstitution.substituteCollocate(): extracts xpos (then falls back to tag) via the same CqlUtils call, builds the identical bracket structure — for collocate injection
Both methods are called on the same RelationConfig.pattern string at different points in the request lifecycle; a change to the constraint merge format must be made in two places
issues.suggestion: Extract the shared [lemma=... & xpos=...] construction into CqlUtils.mergeWithLemma(existingConstraint, lemma) and have both RelationConfig.mergeLemmaConstraint() and PatternSubstitution.substituteCollocate() call it. The tag fallback in PatternSubstitution should be preserved in the shared primitive.

issues.summary: IOException vs IllegalArgumentException → HTTP status mapping is applied consistently in ExplorationHandlers but not in SketchHandlers
evidence: ExplorationHandlers handlers catch IOException → 500 and IllegalArgumentException → 400 separately — typed boundary contract

SketchHandlers.handleBcqlQueryPost(): catches IOException → 500 and Exception → 400; the catch-all Exception for 400 will misclassify any unexpected runtime exception as a bad-request rather than a server error
SketchHandlers.handleFullSketchForType() catches generic Exception inside the relation loop and logs a warning but continues — domain exceptions are swallowed without HTTP reporting
issues.suggestion: Standardise the error propagation contract across all handlers: IOException → 500 (server error), IllegalArgumentException → 400 (client error), other RuntimeException → 500 with logging. Apply this pattern in SketchHandlers.handleBcqlQueryPost() and make the per-relation exception handling in handleFullSketchForType() explicit about which exceptions are swallowable.

root_causes: Model serialization was defined twice because toMap() was added when ExplorationResult was introduced, then handler-side format methods were independently added for response building — neither was made canonical
CQL substitution was implemented independently in config and api packages because the packages evolved separately without a shared utility boundary
